records.ety: [NL., fr. Gr. <?/; pa^s, pa^n, all + kre`as flesh, meat: cf. F. pancras.]

defs: The sweetbread, a gland connected with the intestine of nearly all vertebrates. It is usually elongated and light-colored, and its secretion, called the pancreatic juice, is discharged, often together with the bile, into the upper part of the intestines, and is a powerful aid in digestion. See Illust. of Digestive apparatus.
